title: Expect 100-continue, Early Responses, Body Rejection, and Draining · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs expect 100-continue, early responses, body rejection, and draining from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the expectation and early-response coordination state machine. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for expectation and early-response coordination state machine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse Expect, support 100-continue deliberately, and return appropriate final failure for unsupported expectations.
Send 100 Continue only after header-level policy accepts receiving content and before a final response commits.
Bound client waiting for 100, handle early final responses, and avoid sending content after rejection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Expectation Grammar Policy · changed case · Review Expectation Grammar Policy
steps.instruction:
review case — A review protocol team handles case 6383 in a signed webhook receiver behind an HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 gateway. Build deterministic Go evidence to coordinate 100-continue and bounded early rejection without deadlock or unsafe reuse; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Parse Expect, support 100-continue deliberately, and return appropriate final failure for unsupported expectations.
